summary:
Claim: The mandibular central incisor has the smallest root.  
Evidence: - It features a single, short, and narrow root. - Its root dimensions (length and width) are less than those of any other common tooth.  
Answer: A  

Note: This conclusion aligns with standard dental anatomy references where the mandibular central incisor is consistently cited as having the smallest root.
